source.title: Boredom, sustained attention and the default mode network

confidence_rationale: Direct fMRI study of induced boredom against sustained-attention and resting-state controls. Key finding for R3: boredom and sustained-attention conditions both show anticorrelated anterior insula activity relative to DMN posterior regions, distinguishable from rest. The anterior insula deactivation in boredom is the load-bearing anatomical anchor for routing-channel verdict. Source quality moderate-high (peer-reviewed neuroscience venue, video-based mood induction methodology); mapping fidelity strong for R3 routing; transfer risk moderate because fMRI BOLD signatures translate indirectly to REE substrate routing.
failure_signatures: An ARC-067 substrate that does not anatomically locate the boredom-aversive in an AIC-analog component (i.e., routes the engagement-rate aversive through the same substrate as homeostatic deficit signals like SD-012, rather than through the affective harm stream SD-011) will produce a different network-level signature than Danckert & Merrifield observe -- predicting that an REE-fMRI analog (residue-write distribution, channel utilisation patterns) should not match the boredom signature.

If boredom is implemented through MECH-216 predictive-wanting variants (target-conditioned), the network signature should look more like striatal/vmPFC reward-circuit activity than the AIC-deactivation Danckert observes -- the distinction matters for disambiguation against MECH-216.

mapping.source_claim_statement: fMRI scanning of healthy participants under four conditions (rest, sustained attention task, video-induced boredom, video-induced engagement) revealed that posterior DMN regions showed common correlated activation across all conditions. The boredom-induction and sustained-attention conditions were distinguished from rest by ANTICORRELATED activity in the anterior insula -- when DMN was active, AIC was deactivated. The authors interpret boredom as a failure to engage executive control networks during a monotonous task.
mapping.ree_translation: This is the load-bearing R3 paper for the routing-channel verdict. The AIC-deactivation finding under boredom is consistent with the AIC's role as a salience-and-affective integrator (Critchley lineage): when AIC is deactivated, the agent is not assigning affective salience to the current task, which is exactly the engagement-failure signature ARC-067 names. For ARC-067 routing, this supports an architecture where the boredom aversive routes through the affective harm stream (SD-011 / z_harm_a) -- which is the AIC analog in REE -- and where the LACK of engagement-driven AIC activity is what allows the slow accumulator to fire. The DMN-AIC anticorrelation pattern also suggests that boredom is not just absence of engagement but absence-of-executive-engagement specifically; this favours the Westgate & Wilson 2018 attentional reading on the R1 verdict and constrains the engagement-rate estimator to attentional/executive proxies (E3 deliberation depth being the cleanest REE analog).
mapping.mapping_caveat: fMRI BOLD signals are slow, indirect, and group-averaged -- the AIC-deactivation effect is a population-level signature rather than a mechanistic localisation. The boredom induction was video-based (passive viewing of a boring vs interesting video), which differs from Wilson 2014's enforced-disengagement paradigm and from the open-ended foraging case ARC-067 targets. Translation from BOLD signature to REE substrate routing requires bridging assumptions: that AIC activity in humans corresponds to the affective harm stream in REE (SD-011) and that DMN regions correspond to default-state / no-op-trajectory processing.
mapping.source_context: Danckert is a leading boredom-neuroscience researcher (University of Waterloo) whose lab has produced multiple boredom-fMRI studies. The 2018 paper is the most directly cited fMRI-boredom-DMN-AIC paper in the recent literature.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality solid for fMRI work (Experimental Brain Research is peer-reviewed neuroscience; methodology is video-induced boredom against multiple controls). Mapping fidelity good: AIC-deactivation gives a clean anatomical anchor for the R3 routing-channel verdict. Transfer risk moderately elevated because BOLD-to-REE-substrate mapping requires bridging assumptions about which REE substrates correspond to AIC and DMN.
